fields.lordemannd\n\n 2022-02-24 16:24\n\ndevel:
Gramps 5.1.5 on an English Windows system:
External Persian calendar converters show that entering a Persian date of 1400/11/30 (1400 Bahman 30) should convert to 2022-02-19.

If you enter 2022-02-19 as a Gramps Gregorian date, then change to Persian date, the conversion is wrong,
2022-02-19 converts to 1400/12/00 (1400 Esfand 00),
Gramps preserves the Gregorian date as text string 2022-02-19

If you enter date 1400 Bahman 30 as a Persian calendar date, Gramps will not allow the date to be saved and reports this message at the bottom of the window:
"Correct the date or switch from 'Regular' to 'Text only'"
and won't allow date to be saved.

If you enter Persian calendar date 1400 Bahman 28, save the date, then edit the date, you can add 1 to get day 29 and save it, but add 1 more to become day 30 and Gramps detects it as invalid date.

(for the Persian calendar, the 12th month, Esfand, is the month that gets the leap day added instead of 11th month Bahman)
fields.snoiraud\n\n 2022-02-24 19:30\n\ndevelop:
Could you try to comment out the following line (508) in gramps/gen/lib/gcalendar.py

sdn = math.floor(sdn) + 0.5 #float
sdn = math.floor(sdn) + 0.5 #float

I don't know why we try to round it.
fields.amir\n\n 2022-02-26 12:21\n\nreporter:
Thanks, everyone.
@SNoiraud 's patch works for me too.

fields.lordemannd\n\n 2022-02-27 06:26\n\ndevel:
Works for me with 5.1.5 on Windows.
Even if it isn't included in a future maintenance release, it can be applied as a patch to 5.1.5.

I did a little more testing with the Persian calendar, some strange things that might be general, not just specific to the Persian calendar:

If you set a Gregorian date 2022-03-00, which I think means: about Mar 2022, or an unspecified day within Mar of 2022.
Then using the date editor, change to the Persian calendar: "2022-03-00" is converted to "1399-12-11 (Persian)".
This seems wrong, the approximate date now seems to be a precise date, I'm not sure what is right.

in the date editor, if you try to change 1399-12-11 (1399 Esfand 11) to 1399-12-00, by entering 0 into the day field, it is not allowed,
but you can use the "-" minus button to decrement to 0.

Esfand (12th month) is the month that gets the leap day, it has 29 or 30 days; in the date editor, if you try to change 1399-12-11 (1399 Esfand 11) to 1399-12-29, you can enter the 29 or use the plus button, if you enter day 33 (which is invalid) the date editor changes it to 31 (which is also invalid) instead of 29 or 30 (in a Persian leap year).

later addition:

if you use the word "about" in the date the same thing happens, the approximate date gets converted to a precise date
In Persian calendar enter "about 1399", then change to Gregorian calendar, you get 2020-03-20 a precise date.
fields.snoiraud\n\n 2022-02-28 07:48\n\ndevelop:
If you set a Gregorian date 2022-03-00, which I think means: about Mar 2022, or an unspecified day within Mar of 2022.
Then using the date editor, change to the Persian calendar: "2022-03-00" is converted to "1399-12-11 (Persian)".
This seems wrong, the approximate date now seems to be a precise date, I'm not sure what is right.

It is converted to "Esfand 10, 1400 (Persan)" for me or "1400-12-10 (Persan)" with the patch.
If I convert this date in gregorian, I get "2022-03-01" ?

in the date editor, if you try to change 1399-12-11 (1399 Esfand 11) to 1399-12-00, by entering 0 into the day field, it is not allowed,
but you can use the "-" minus button to decrement to 0.

I can enter a zero in the day field. Ig get "1399-12-00 (Persan)" without using the minus button.

Esfand (12th month) is the month that gets the leap day, it has 29 or 30 days; in the date editor, if you try to change 1399-12-11 (1399 > Esfand 11) to 1399-12-29, you can enter the 29 or use the plus button, if you enter day 33 (which is invalid) the date editor changes it
to 31 (which is also invalid) instead of 29 or 30 (in a Persian leap year).

A problem here.

I guess the author of this bug request knows the Persian calendar better than us.
Can he test and let us know?

If you enter date 1400 Bahman 30 as a Persian calendar date, Gramps will not allow the date to be saved and reports this message at the bottom of the window:
"Correct the date or switch from 'Regular' to 'Text only'"

If you enter Persian calendar date 1400 Bahman 28, save the date, then edit the date, you can add 1 to get day 29 and save it, but add 1 more to become day 30 and Gramps detects it as invalid date.

(for the Persian calendar, the 12th month, Esfand, is the month that gets the leap day added instead of 11th month Bahman)

Esfand (12th month) is the month that gets the leap day, it has 29 or 30 days; in the date editor, if you try to change 1399-12-11 (1399 Esfand 11) to 1399-12-29, you can enter the 29 or use the plus button, if you enter day 33 (which is invalid) the date editor changes it to 31 (which is also invalid) instead of 29 or 30 (in a Persian leap year).
